channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Sony temporarily suspends memory card sales due to shortages
item.description: Sony has announced that as of March 27th, 2026, the company is no longer accepting orders for nearly all the products in its CFexpress and SD memory card lines. The list of affected memory products includes CFexpress Type A, Type B, and SDXC/SDHC cards, although a few models of Type B and low-end SF-UZ series […]

item.title: Verizon waives late fees for federal workers affected by partial DHS shutdown
item.description: Verizon will waive late fees and offer flexible payment arrangements for workers affected by the partial government shutdown. The carrier has made similar offers in the past, like during the COVID-19 pandemic when it gave customers extra mobile data at no additional cost. The Department of Homeland Security has been hit the hardest by the partial shutdown, but Verizon's offer covers any federal worker who's able to offer employment verification. Verizon says employees can call 1-800-Verizon (1-800-922-0204) to get their late fees waived and set up a payment plan.The partial government shutdown...

item.title: The best gaming deals in the Amazon Big Spring Sale: Shop PS5, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch
item.description: Shop gaming deals live Amazon's Big Spring Sale, including gaming monitors and video games for PS5, Xbox, Nintendo Switch.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Saturday, March 28 (game #1524)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Saturday, March 28 (game #755)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Saturday, March 28 (game #1021)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: AI Research Is Getting Harder to Separate From Geopolitics
item.description: A policy change announced by NeurIPS, the world’s leading AI research conference, drew widespread backlash from Chinese researchers this week and then was quickly reversed.

item.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
item.description: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

item.title: 'The next evolution of the Arm compute platform': AGI CPU is Arm's first in-house AI chip, Meta and OpenAI sign up as early clients — and it might have picked the perfect time to launch
item.description: Arm introduces its first in-house AGI CPU, offering high-density performance and gaining early backing from major companies, including Meta and OpenAI.

item.title: Kash Patel's personal email account was accessed by hackers linked to Iran
item.description: A hacking group called Handala has gained access to FBI Director Kash Patel's email account, Reuters reports. The group published content from Patel's email on their website as proof, including photos of Patel "sniffing and smoking cigars" and "making a face while taking a picture of himself in the mirror with a ​large bottle of rum." TechCrunch was able to independently confirm that at least some of the emails Handala stole were from Patel's account by checking information used by mail delivery systems that’s stored in an email's header. Several stolen emails included a cryptographic signatur...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg offered to 'help' Elon Musk with DOGE in 2025
item.description: Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg have a complicated history. In 2023, the two vowed to fight each other in a cage match that never happened. But by early 2025, when both were cozying up to the newly-elected President Donald Trump, they were apparently on more friendly terms. In February of that year, Zuckerberg texted Musk approvingly about his work with the now-defunct Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). "Looks like DOGE is making progress," the Meta CEO texted. "I've got our teams on alert to take down content doxxing or threatening the people on your team. Let me know if there's anythi...

item.title: Epstein Victims Sue Google, Claim AI Mode Exposed Personal Information
item.description: Google's AI republished sensitive info like contact information, the suit claims.

item.title: ‘Wicked Spot’ Is a Fun, Sapphic Rom-Com That Yeets a Witch Into the Magical World of Influencer Culture
item.description: If you like 'Green Yuri,' check out Sal Jiang's new manga about a witch's foray into the magic of social media likes, thirst traps, and an enemies-to-lovers relationship with her internet troll.

item.title: How to watch USA vs. Belgium online for free
item.description: How to watch live football for free. Live stream USA vs. Belgium in a 2026 International Friendly for free.

item.title: White House launches mobile app, includes live feeds and ICE reporting line
item.description: The Trump administration announces new White House mobile app, includes social media feeds, press releases, and even an ICE reporting line.

item.title: Anthropic and OpenAI Just Gave Us a Glimpse Into the Future of Model Pricing
The Best Office Chair Is $50 Cheaper Than We've Seen Before
item.description: The Branch Ergonomic Chair Pro offers great value at full price—but right now, it's $100 off.

item.title: 10 Things You Can Do While Waiting in the TSA Line
item.description: As Washington wrangles over the future of federal employees’ pay, here are some ideas for passing the time.
